preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for a Telegram channel adapter derived from CLV-018 roadmap records.

acceptance_criteria.text: Define Telegram channel contracts for bot outbound messages, inbound updates, callback queries, media metadata, delivery/failure state, disabled states, and credential validation.
Implement secure bot-token storage and webhook secret handling with no token leakage through renderer, logs, diagnostics, exports, or screenshots.
Add explicit contact linking for Telegram chat IDs/handles so no Telegram identity gains trust, approval, or urgent-interrupt privileges automatically.
Implement outbound text behind the channel registry with deterministic tests for send success, provider rejection, missing credentials, rate limit, disabled state, and malformed recipient.
Implement inbound update normalization with duplicate handling, webhook validation, bounded text/media metadata, contact resolution, and blocked/muted policy enforcement.
If inline buttons or callback quick actions ship, require signed callback payloads, expiration, replay protection, local pending-action lookup, and durable audit outcomes.
Add UI for Telegram setup, allowed chats/contact linking, disabled/planned state, test message, quick-action redaction preview, and provider error display.
Document setup, privacy limitations, bot-mode caveats, failure modes, and what is shipped versus deferred.
